headlines.title: Trump imposing 50% tariffs on certain Canadian goods over alleged trade discrimination
headlines.summary: Canada's relationship with the U.S. has frayed amid Trump's tariff-heavy agenda and vocal displeasure with the nations' trilateral trade pact with Mexico.

headlines.title: Oil prices rise after Trump says Iran will pay for killing U.S. service members
headlines.summary: Oil prices have surged around 20% this month as fighting between the U.S. and Iran has sharply escalated.

headlines.title: The FDA made a big reversal in its cyclosporiasis probe, but it still thinks iceberg lettuce is to blame
headlines.summary: The FDA's investigation into the source of the cyclosporiasis outbreak is causing confusion as companies and consumers alike navigate new updates.

headlines.title: Trump says Netanyahu won't be arrested in New York, pushing back on Mamdani
headlines.summary: Israel's prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, is the target of an arrest warrant issued by the International Criminal Court related to the war on Gaza.

headlines.title: Paramount and Warner Bros. merger hit with temporary restraining order
headlines.summary: A California judge granted a temporary restraining order as part of the lawsuit brought by state attorneys general due to antitrust concerns.

headlines.title: Trump administration's head of AI safety agency resigns after 3 months on job
headlines.summary: Arvind Raman, the director of National Institute of Standards and Technology, will serve as acting director of CAISI, according to a spokesperson

headlines.title: Top Wall Street analysts suggest these 3 dividend stocks for steady income
headlines.summary: Here are three dividend-paying stocks that are highlighted by Wall Street's top pros, as tracked by TipRanks.

headlines.title: Kalshi adds 3 million new users as prediction market capitalizes on the World Cup
headlines.summary: The prediction market has launched some ads featuring soccer players, as well as formed a partnership to display its brand in more places during the tournament.

headlines.title: Two unexpected revelations in Warren Buffett's CNBC interview
headlines.summary: Warren Buffett tells CNBC that Bill Gates' ties to Jeffrey Epstein didn't drive his termination of donations.

headlines.title: How are prediction markets taxed? The IRS hasn’t provided guidance yet
headlines.summary: Experts say the lack of federal guidance makes it's unclear on how prediction markets winnings should be reported and levied.

headlines.title: Short sellers load up against SpaceX as stock drops below IPO price
headlines.summary: About 185 million SpaceX shares are now sold short, representing roughly 29% of the company's publicly tradable float, according to S3.

headlines.title: Historic IBM stock crash sets up unique options strategy
headlines.summary: IBM shares fell just over $73 to approximately $217 — a jaw-dropping 25% single-day decapitation.

headlines.title: Why are gasoline prices rising faster than oil prices? Blame it on the ‘crack.’
headlines.summary: The spread between the cost of barrels of gasoline and crude oil — known as the “crack spread” — has widened sharply.

headlines.title: I will claim Social Security early. Why do so few people talk about the elephant in the room?
headlines.summary: “The strongest argument for claiming benefits earlier goes beyond the traditional break-even analyses.”

headlines.title: Why cheap Chinese AI models could actually be a boon for Nvidia, Micron and other chip stocks
headlines.summary: Moonshot AI’s Kimi K3 could encourage a surge in enterprise workloads and provide a long-term tailwind to chip demand.

headlines.title: We have $8 million in traditional IRAs. Should we tap them to buy a house — and take the tax hit?
headlines.summary: “We would rather pay taxes upfront than pay mortgage interest.”

headlines.title: U.S. hits Canada with stiff new tariffs, escalating trade tensions
headlines.summary: The Trump administration on Monday said it would slap 50% tariffs on some goods imported from Canada, in an effort to end what it sees as discriminatory trade practices by Ottawa against the U.S. automobile, dairy and alcohol industries.

headlines.title: Americans are skipping lettuce and salads as investigation into cyclospora outbreak continues
headlines.summary: Shares of salad chains like Cava, Chipotle and Sweetgreen closed lower on Monday.

observations: High concentration in Communication Services, particularly in META, which poses a risk to portfolio diversification.
Underperformance in positions like OKLO, NFLX, IREN, FBL, and RUN indicating potential need for reevaluation or trimming.
Cash position at 9.3% exceeds the minimum cash buffer requirement of 5%, allowing for potential redeployment into more favorable sectors.
suggested_changes: Trim META by ~6 share(s) to bring weight under 25%
Consider reallocating excess cash into sectors aligned with investor preferences, such as AI / compute infrastructure or clean energy.
open_questions: What is the investor's stance on holding underperforming positions like OKLO, NFLX, IREN, FBL, and RUN?
Is there a preference for specific sectors or themes to focus on for potential new investments?
